version_format: YYYY.MM.DD — date-based versioning. Bump this and .ruyos-version together on every release.
description: ruyOS vault scaffold — the single source of truth for setup and update skills

content_files._comment: Files that exist as actual files in the repo. setup-ruyos copies these to the vault. repo_path is where to find them in the repo, install_path is where they go in the vault.

obsidian._comment: Obsidian config and community plugins. Copied as a directory tree. skip_if_exists prevents overwriting user customizations on update.

generated_files._comment: Files generated at install time from these definitions. Not stored as files in the repo.

files.heading: AI setup
files.body:
How ruyOS works as an AI-powered second brain.

## Philosophy

The vault is the single source of truth. AI agents (Claude via Cowork, Claude Code, or any other provider) read and write to this vault. The CLAUDE.md file at the root is the navigation layer that tells any AI agent how to find and store information.

## Key components

- **CLAUDE.md** — Brain file with knowledge routing and save routing tables
- **Context/Personal/** — Identity, goals, preferences, people
- **Context/Professional/** — Work, strategy, brand, writing style, ICP, tech stack
- **Workflow/Intelligence/** — Accumulated knowledge from meetings, decisions, research
- **Settings/Resources/** — Reusable prompts, frameworks, examples
- **Workflow/Tasks/** — AI-readable task tracking

## Skills

ruyOS ships with workflow skills in `.claude/skills/`:

| Skill | What it does |
|-------|-------------|
| **start-day** | Morning briefing and daily note creation |
| **end-day** | Daily wrap-up, archive, and reflection |
| **end-session** | Checkpoint after a work block or iteration |
| **remember** | Save information to the right vault location |
| **inbox** | Triage items in Workflow/Capture/ to permanent homes |
| **review** | Realign priorities against goals and focus |
| **setup-ruyos** | Bootstrap a fresh vault |
| **update-ruyos** | Pull latest updates from the ruyOS repo |

Skills reference vault context instead of embedding copies. One update to a context file benefits every skill.

## Tips

- Every new Cowork/Claude Code session should point to this vault folder
- When the AI learns a rule or preference, tell it to save it to the right place
- If navigation breaks, update CLAUDE.md — it's the bridge
- Put unsorted captures in Workflow/Capture/ and process later

files.heading: Resources
files.body:
Reusable building blocks — prompts, frameworks, examples, and snippets. Think of this as a library that skills and agents can pull from.

## Subfolders

- **Prompts/** — Proven prompts for recurring tasks
- **Frameworks/** — Mental models, decision frameworks, analysis templates
- **Examples/** — Good output examples (posts, emails, presentations) for AI reference
- **Snippets/** — Reusable code snippets, text blocks, email templates

## How to use

When building a skill, point it to resources here instead of embedding copies. When you produce something good, save an example here for future reference.

files.heading: Skill template
files.body:
> [!info] Purpose
> Use this template when creating new skills for Claude Code or Cowork. The key principle: skills should point to vault context, not embed their own copies.

## Template

```markdown
---
name: skill-name
description: "When to trigger this skill. Be specific about trigger phrases and use cases."
---

# Skill Name

One-sentence description of what the skill produces or automates.

## Context sources

Read these files from the vault before executing:

- Context/Professional/Writing Preferences.md
- Context/Professional/ICP.md
- [Add other relevant context files]

## What to do

1. [Step 1 — what to do first]
2. [Step 2 — what to do next]
3. [Step 3 — and so on]

## Output format

[Describe what the output should look like — length, structure, tone.]

## Rules

- Follow all rules in Context/Professional/Writing Preferences.md
- [Add skill-specific rules here]
```

## Why this pattern

- **No duplicated context.** When you update Writing Preferences once, every skill that references it gets the update.
- **Faster skill creation.** You only write the process, not the context.
- **Consistent output.** All skills pull from the same voice and style definitions.

files.raw_content:
---
title: "{{ date }}"
tags:
  - daily
date: "{{ date }}"
---

# {{ date }}

## Focus

What I'm working on today. Pull from [[Active]] tasks.

-

## Log

What happened, what I worked on, key events.

-

## Decisions

Any decisions made today. Copy significant ones to [[Intelligence/Decisions/Decisions|Decision Log]].

-

## Learned

Things I learned or insights from today.

-

## Tomorrow

What to carry forward.

-

---

**Date:** {{ date }}
**Attendees:**
**Context:**

-

tags:
  - project
status: planning

last-updated: "{{ date }}"
directory: "{{ directory_name }}"
context-dependencies: []
---

## Vision

What is this project and why does it exist?

## Goals

-

Current state of the project.

## Log

## Session notes

```dataview
TABLE date AS "Date", title AS "Summary"
FROM "Projects/{{ project_name }}"
WHERE contains(tags, "session")
SORT date DESC
```

files.heading: Context
files.body:
Who you are, what you do, and how you work. This is the identity layer that every skill draws from.

## Personal

- [[About Me]] — The basics: name, location, values
- [[Current Focus]] — What you're actively working on
- [[Goals]] — Short and long-term objectives
- [[Interests & Hobbies]] — Life outside work
- [[People]] — Key people in your life and work
- [[Preferences]] — How you like things done

## Professional

- [[Work]] — Current employer, role, team
- [[Work & Career]] — Career history and skills
- [[Strategy]] — Where you're headed and why
- [[Writing Preferences]] — Voice, tone, and style rules
- [[Communication Style]] — How you communicate across channels
- [[Brand]] — Personal and professional brand identity
- [[ICP]] — Who you're writing for
- [[Pain Points]] — What your audience struggles with
- [[Tech Stack]] — Languages, tools, and platforms

files.heading: Workflow
files.body:
The engine room — where daily work, tasks, and knowledge accumulate.

## Areas

- [[Active]] — Current to-do list
- [[Backlog]] — Things to do eventually
- [[Done]] — Completed tasks archive
- [[Intelligence]] — Accumulated knowledge from meetings, decisions, research

## Folders

- **Capture/** — Inbox for unsorted items. Process with `/inbox`
- **Daily/** — Daily notes created by `/start-day`
- **Tasks/** — Task tracking files
- **Intelligence/** — Meetings, decisions, research, insights

files.heading: Projects
files.body:
Active and past projects. Each project gets its own subfolder with a project file and session notes.

## How projects work

Create a new project with the **New Project** template. Each project folder contains a project file (goals, status, log) and session notes from individual work blocks.

```dataview
TABLE status AS "Status", last-updated AS "Updated"
FROM "Projects"
WHERE contains(tags, "project")
SORT last-updated DESC
```

files.heading: Settings
files.body:
System configuration, templates, and reusable resources. The infrastructure layer.

## Areas

- [[AI Setup]] — How ruyOS works as an AI-powered second brain
- [[Computer Overview]] — Hardware, software, and dev environment
- [[Resources]] — Reusable prompts, frameworks, examples, snippets

## Templates

Starter templates used by skills and Templater:

- **Daily Note** — Created by `/start-day`
- **Meeting Note** — For meeting capture
- **New Project** — Project scaffolding
- **Session Note** — Work block documentation
- **Weekly Review** — Weekly reflection

files.heading: ruyOS
files.body:
Welcome to the vault. This is the operating system for your life, work, and projects — powered by Obsidian and AI.

Run `/start-day` to begin, or explore the folders to set up your context.

files.heading: Intelligence
files.body:
Accumulated knowledge — meetings, decisions, research, and insights. This is the vault's long-term memory.

## Subfolders

- **Meetings/** — Meeting transcripts and notes. Format: `YYYY-MM-DD <meeting-name>.md`
- **Decisions/** — Significant decisions with rationale, context, and date
- **Research/** — Market research, competitor analysis, technology evaluations
- **Insights/** — Observations, patterns, things learned over time

files.heading: Decision log
files.body:
Significant decisions with rationale. Newest first.

<!-- Format for new entries:
## YYYY-MM-DD — Decision title
**Context:** Why this came up
**Decision:** What was decided
**Rationale:** Why this over alternatives
**Impact:** What changed as a result
-->

files.heading: Writing preferences
files.body:
Tone, style, and rules for all written output. AI agents must follow these.

## Voice

-

## Tone

-

## Living rules

Rules that accumulate over time. Add new ones as you discover preferences.

- Active voice preferred
- No filler words (just, simply, basically, actually)
- Clear over clever — say what you mean
- Sentence case for headings
- American English spelling

## Things to avoid

-

system.note: Optional — required for document creation skills (docx, pptx, xlsx, pdf)
